title: mfa — helper functions
description: Two helpers. Both live at Support tier so anyone can call them without pulling in the bindings — the underlying flows go through the container.

helpers.purpose: Convenience accessor for gated UI. Returns true when the resolved caller has completed an MFA challenge within `config('mfa.step_up.sensitive_action_ttl_minutes')`. Reads the session's `last_mfa_challenge_at`.
behaviours: Returns false when the Identity has no MFA enrolled — the concept of a 'recent challenge' is undefined
Returns false when there is no active session (CLI, background jobs)
Cached in the request-scoped container so repeated calls in one request don't reread the session

helpers.purpose: Convenience accessor. Returns the count of unconsumed recovery codes for the resolved caller.
behaviours: Returns 0 when the Identity has no MFA enrolled OR the recovery-codes array is null
Reads the cached posture from the `mfa:posture:{identity_id}` Redis key when available; falls through to Postgres otherwise
This is a read of the on-Identity JSONB array count — no plaintext codes flow through this helper

notes: Helpers are registered by the composer autoloader from `src/Support/helpers.php`. Namespaced functions are safe to call across every framework layer including migrations + seeders (though these two are session-aware and will return conservative defaults in migration / seeder context).

There is deliberately NO helper for issuing / verifying a challenge — those operations MUST go through the DI-bound services (TotpChallenger / WebauthnChallenger / RecoveryCodeChallenger) so timing constants are preserved. A helper that shortcut'd verification would have to duplicate the constant-time compare + audit log integration + suspicious-activity signal emission — that's the challenger's job.

Similarly no helper for `mfa_enabled(?Identity $identity = null)` — that concept is on identity/identity's Identity model (`$identity->mfa_enabled` accessor). Cross-module accessor duplication is anti-pattern.
